what_it_does: Implements a cross-cutting surface navigator that lets the user switch between the map/home and overlay surfaces (decisions, fabric, board, sessions, transcript). It tries to render an external DNA navRail into a host div and wire tile clicks to surface open handlers; if the DNA renderer is absent or throws, fallback labelled buttons still open the surfaces.
observations: Defines a static SURFACES array mapping ids to labels, optional icons, and imperative open callbacks.
Opens surfaces by dispatching window CustomEvents (channels:open, board:open, sessions:open, transcript:open) or a synthetic Escape keydown for the map.
Opens the decisions surface by calling the imported openDecisionsList function.
Attempts to render DNA.org.navRail by writing its returned HTML string into a host div and attaching a native click listener.
Always renders a fallback set of plain labelled buttons so navigation works even when DNA is not loaded or navRail throws.
Uses the stamp utility to attach ui:// addresses to the container and each fallback button.
Includes extensive comments explaining the DNA-host separation, Tim's gap, and degrade-clean requirements.
standouts: Degrade-clean dual rendering: DNA navRail when available, permanent React fallback otherwise.
Event-driven surface opening via global window events rather than direct component imports.
Explicit design-substrate hosting pattern where an external renderer provides HTML and the local component provides semantics and behavior.
conventions: Follows React functional component conventions with hooks, but uses useEffect for imperative DOM manipulation.
Uses TypeScript type aliases for local contracts and a loosely typed global window extension.
Uses a module-level constant array for configuration.
Breaks typical React declarative rendering by assigning innerHTML from an external function and adding a native event listener.
Uses a global event bus (window CustomEvent) for cross-surface communication.
concerns: Synthetic Escape keydown may not satisfy listeners that inspect event details or require bubbling/composed behavior, and could trigger unrelated global Escape handlers.
innerHTML assignment from an external string bypasses React reconciliation and could introduce XSS if DNA.navRail returns untrusted HTML.
The fallback buttons remain in the DOM even when DNA navRail renders; whether they are hidden or duplicated depends on CSS not shown here.
Native click listener is added only on mount; the component does not clean up the innerHTML on unmount.
byId silently no-ops for unknown data-nav ids because of optional chaining.
DNAGlobal typing is weak (unknown options), so navRail misuse is not caught at compile time.
SURFACES is a mutable module-level array; accidental mutation would affect all consumers.
notes: A resilient surface switcher that wires an optional DNA nav rail to event-driven surface openers, with a permanent React button fallback.
questions: Does CSS visually hide the fallback buttons when the DNA navRail renders, or are both sets of controls shown?
Which components subscribe to channels:open, board:open, sessions:open, transcript:open, and how do they handle duplicate or out-of-order events?
Is openDecisionsList safe to call repeatedly and does it manage its own overlay state?
What HTML and data-nav contract does DNA.org.navRail actually produce, and does it match the SURFACES ids?
Will synthetic Escape events be correctly recognized by all overlay close handlers?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file's stated purpose is to link all faces and degrade cleanly, and the implementation does exactly that by wiring DNA navRail tiles (or fallback buttons) to surface openers.
intention_signals: Provide a single, always-available navigation rail for all application surfaces, leveraging a shared design system's navRail when present but never depending on it for functionality.
novelty: The explicit DNA-host pattern: an external design function returns HTML, the local host attaches behavior via data-nav attributes, and a React fallback is always kept in the DOM.
contribution: A surface-navigation host that decouples visual rendering from action wiring, ensuring the operator can always reach every surface even when the external design substrate is missing or fails.
summary_for_embedding: SurfaceNav.tsx is a React surface navigator that renders an optional DNA navRail and dispatches window events to open sibling overlays, falling back to labelled buttons if the design substrate is unavailable.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to give the operator a reliable, always-present way to switch between the map and the fabric, board, sessions, decisions, and transcript surfaces.

symbols.does: Type alias describing a navigable surface with id, human label, optional icon, and an open callback.
symbols.description: Defines the contract for entries in the SURFACES list; each surface must have a string id, a human-readable label, an optional icon name, and a zero-argument open function that triggers the surface.

symbols.does: Type alias for the optional global DNA design object that may expose org.navRail.
symbols.description: Describes the expected shape of window.DNA: an optional org property that may contain a navRail renderer returning an HTML string; used only for type narrowing when accessing the external design substrate.

symbols.does: Accessor that returns the DNA navRail renderer from the global window object, if present.
symbols.description: A small helper that safely reaches into window.DNA?.org?.navRail so the component can detect whether the external design substrate is loaded and callable.

symbols.does: React component that renders the surface navigation rail and wires each tile to its surface opener.
symbols.description: Mounts an optional DNA navRail into a host div via innerHTML, attaches a click listener that maps data-nav attributes to SURFACES open callbacks, and always renders a fallback set of labelled buttons so navigation survives missing or broken DNA.

symbols.does: Looks up a surface by id and returns its open callback.
symbols.description: Used by the DNA navRail click handler to resolve a data-nav tile id into the corresponding surface action; returns undefined for unknown ids.

symbols.does: Click handler inside the DNA navRail host that delegates tile taps to surface openers.
symbols.description: Finds the nearest element with a data-nav attribute in the clicked target, reads its id, and invokes the matching surface's open function from SURFACES.
